other_observers: Paul & Fran Didier; Originally found and identified by Paul Didier and me. We are both experienced observers and familiar with the bird.  Rich Levad, among others, also saw this bird.
specific_location: at approx. MP54.8 on CO Hwy. 65.
description: Black head and breast. Black upperparts. White belly and undertail coverts, the white forming a point into the black breast. Black straight flycatcher bill. Dark eye.Behaviors: Bird in small stream channel west of Plateau Creek, perched in low bushes and making flycatching sorties, then returning to same or nearby perch. It flicked its tail frequently while perched, in the manner of a phoebe.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
